original: Five-month-old bar-tailed godwit becomes a world record holder by flying 8,425 miles non-stop from Alaska to Australia in 11 days; bird departed Yukon Kuskokwim Delta in Alaska, US, on October 13 and touched down in Ansons Bay in Tasmania, Australia
A camera on board the V-2 No. 13 rocket, launched from Whites Sands US, takes the first photograph of earth from outer space.

Mt. Vesuvius erupts, burying the cities of Pompeii, Herculaneum, Oplontis and Stabiae and killing thousands. New research in 2018 suggests the eruption occurred at about this date, not the previously used 24 August.
First woman to go over Niagara Falls in a barrel (Anna Taylor)
Orville Wrightremains in the air for 9 minutes and 45 seconds in a glider at Kill Devil Hills, North Carolina, setting a new world record that stands for 10 years
Alleged teleportation of Spanish soldier Gil Perez from the Philippines to Mexico
Largest jackpot in US history at $1.6 billion won by one person in South Carolina
Billy Martinpunches a marshmallow salesman, puts job in jeopardy
Hasan Raza makes Test Cricket debut for Pakistan age 14 yrs 238 days
Felix Mendelssohn, aged 9, performs his first public concert in Berlin, Germany
Bob Kane, American comic artist and Batman co-creator, born in New York City (d. 1998)
Scientists confirm East Island in Hawaii, half a mile long, has been wiped out after contact with Hurricane Walaka
William Lassell discovers Ariel & Umbriel, satellites of Uranus
Launch of Deep Space 1 on comet and asteroid mission
Annie Edson Taylor, American schoolteacher and first person to survive a trip over Niagara Falls in a barrel, born in Auburn, New York (d. 1921)
Polina Gelman, Soviet member of the "Night Witches" all-female aviator unit during World War Ⅱ, born in Berdichev, Ukraine (d. 2005)[1]
Julius Youngner, American scientist, helped develop Salk polio vaccine, born in Manhattan, New York (d. 2017)
EU directive bans single-use plastics by 2021
Santa Maria volcano in Guatemala erupts, killing 6,000 people and becoming one of the three largest eruptions of the 20th century
Strongest storm to ever hit the West Coast of the US, with California recording barometric pressure of 945.2 mb, and San Francisco its wettest October day
Harry Houdini's last performance, at the Garrick Theatre in Detroit, Michigan
Nathaniel Wyeth, American chemist and inventor (created PET plastic beverage bottle), born in Chadds Ford, Pennsylvania (d. 1990)
Concorde makes its last commercial flight
The last high-altitude X-15 flight, piloted by William Dana, reaches an altitude of 77.7 km
CaptainRobert Falcon Scott's expedition leaves Cape Evans for South Pole
Tom Bradybecomes first quarterback in NFL history to record 600 touchdown passes when he hits Mike Evans in 1st quarter of the Buccaneers 38-3 rout of the Chicago Bears in Tampa Bay
Joel Embiidbecomes the first NBA player to reach 30 points and 19 rebounds in the same game since Charles Barkley in 1991
Dog doping scandal confirmed by officials of Iditarod, Alaskan dog sled race after dogs test positive for a banned substance
Women in Iceland go on strike to protest at gender inequalities, including its Prime Minister Katrín Jakobsdóttir
US Mint issues first US currency featuring an Asian-American - silent film actress Anna May Wong; coin is part of American Women Quarters Program celebrates female trailblazers
Albert Einstein's "Theory of Happiness", written as a note for a bellboy instead of a tip in Tokyo in 1922 sells for $1.56 million
US forbids child labor in factories
Recognized by FIFA as the oldest existing club still playing football in the world, Sheffield FC is founded in Yorkshire, England; now based in Dronfield, Derbyshire
Manchester United beats Arsenal, 2-0 at Old Trafford; ends Gunners' English Premier League record 49-game unbeaten streak
Anthony Christian, English child prodigy artist and portrait painter, born in London
Kenyan marathon runnerEliud Kipchogenamed UN Person of the Year for setting a new marathon world record and for his work with AIDS/HIV in Kenya
Total solar eclipse in SW/S Asia (2m09s)
The spectacular Cathedral of Chartres is dedicated in the presence of KingLouis IXof France; now a UNESCO World Heritage Site
Australian swimmer Michael Wendon wraps up the Mexico City Games sprint double when he wins the men's 200m freestyle gold medal in Olympic record 1:55.2
English McLaren driver James Hunt finishes 4th in the season ending Japanese Grand Prix at Fuji Speedway to win his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by 1 point fromNiki Lauda
Wuxia martial arts film "Hero" directed by Zhang Yimou, starringJet Lireleased in China (US release 2004)
Walt Disney Concert Hall, new home of the Los Angeles Philharmonic, designed byFrank Gehry, opens in downtown Los Angeles, California
MESSENGER spacecraft performs a Venus flyby
Nobel prize for physiology or medicine awarded to Dutchman Willem Einthoven "for his discovery of the mechanism of the electrocardiogram"
Marjorie Stewart Joyner, American hair and beauty entrepreneur (invented permanent wave machine) and activist, born in Monterey, Virginia (d. 1994)[1]
Steffi Grafplays her 1st pro tennis match
New York Islanders and NHL high scorer,Mike Bossy(31) retires

Britain's Mike Hawthorne wins season ending Spanish Grand Prix at Pedralbes; Argentine Maserati driverJuan Manuel Fangiotakes second Formula 1 World Drivers Championship by 17 points from countryman José Froilán González
Pablo Picasso's 1937 painting Guernica goes on display in Madrid Spain to celebrate the centenary of the artist's birth
Guinness Book of Records presentsPaul McCartneywith a rhodium disc as all-time best selling singer-songwriter

Jim Clarkin a Lotus is forced to retire from season-ending Mexican Grand Prix at Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez with engine trouble; American Richie Ginther is race winner and Clark claims his second F1 World Drivers Championship
Benny Goodmanrecords "Let's Dance"

Edward Meeker hits the charts with "Take Me Out to the Ball Game"; songwriters Edward Albert Von Tilzer and Jack Norworth had yet to attend a baseball game, the 1st recording is often misattributed to singer Billy Murray
NHL Toronto Maple Leafs set own team record of 28 pts vs NY Islanders
Test cricket debut of Pakistani standouts Asif Iqbal, Majid Khan and Khalid Ibadulla in drawn 1st Test vs Australia in Karachi; Khalid "Billy" Ibadulla scores 166 on debut
Zambia (formerly Northern Rhodesia) gains independence from Britain (National Day) with Kenneth Kaunda becoming President
Hurricane Sandy makes landfall in Jamaica killing 1 person and causing over $50 million in damage
Napoleonic Wars: The Battle of Maloyaroslavets takes place near Moscow, French army then forced to retreat through the snow towards Smolensk
Levi P Morton, US ambassador to France drives first rivet in Statue of Liberty
Earliest American patent for a phosphorus friction match by Alonzo Dwight Phillips of Springfield, Massachusetts
